qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via webhook subscriber URL without internal IP validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: webhook subscriber URL validation and outbound webhook dispatch, not guest checkout address ID assignment or address ownership validation.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: TOCTOU race condition in digital download authorization bypasses access limits
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: non-atomic DigitalLink#authorize! download counter update, not checkout update/address book assignment of guest bill_address_id or ship_address_id.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described guest checkout IDOR root cause: plain bill_address_id and ship_address_id parameters are permitted and assigned through checkout/address book logic without the ownership validation applied to nested address attributes.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-87fh-rc96-6fr6 concerns Spree guest checkout address assignment: core/lib/spree/permitted_attributes.rb permits bill_address_id and ship_address_id, core/app/services/spree/checkout/update.rb validates only nested address attributes, and core/app/models/spree/order/address_book.rb accepts guest addresses where address.user_id and order.user_id are both nil.

The ssrf-via-webhook-subscriber-url finding concerns Spree::UrlValidator and Spree::Webhooks::Subscribers::MakeRequest accepting internal webhook subscriber URLs and issuing outbound Net::HTTP requests.
The toctou-digital-download-limit-bypass finding concerns Spree::DigitalLink#authorize! performing a non-atomic authorizable? check followed by increment! for digital download access limits.
